name: loop-kit
description: Turn-gated loop plugin that keeps one turn alive until explicit user stop and selects the right internal loop mode from turn-gate.

interface.shortDescription: Turn-gated continuity with internal execution, refinement, review, and readiness loops driven from one main surface.
interface.longDescription: Provides a narrow operational plugin where turn-gate is the main user-facing surface. It keeps one turn alive until the user explicitly ends the work, runs each flow through preparation, work, verification, and reporting, uses deep-interview plus flow-list design for user-message-driven preparation, collects enough initial information, expected risky actions, and approval boundaries to execute the planned flow list, inspects current state and scope for existing-flow preparation, selects the right internal loop mode for autonomous execution, refinement, review findings, or readiness, and provides turn-gate-self-drive as an overlay for running prepared planned flows through bounded subagent question routing until final commit-readiness reporting.
defaultPrompt: Keep this turn alive until I explicitly stop and reopen the next flow with explicit choices.
Run each turn-gated flow as preparation, work, verification, and reporting.
For user-message preparation, use deep-interview alignment and turn the result into a planned flow list before execution.

Collect enough information during user-message preparation to execute planned flows with clear boundaries, non-goals, verification expectations, expected risky actions, and user-gated approval checkpoints.
For existing-flow preparation, inspect scope, current state, target files, and verification expectations before work.
Use turn-gate as the main controller and choose the right internal loop for the current phase.
Handle this as a turn-gated loop where autonomous execution, refinement, review, and readiness modes are selected inside turn-gate.
Use turn-gate-self-drive when prepared planned flows should continue through bounded subagent decisions without waiting for me.
When self-driving a planned flow sequence, end the last flow with commit-readiness reporting; do not treat it as commit execution approval.
Do not expose direct loop entrypoints; drive the work through turn-gate.
